title: catha cassinoides
intro:
Catha cassinoides is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Celastraceae family, and is also known as the 'false khat' due to its structural similarities to the well-known khat plant (Catha edulis).

Catha cassinoides has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues. Its leaves contain compounds with adaptogenic and stimulant effects, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or smoked to release its active compounds, and it has been reported to have a range of health benefits, including improved cognitive function, reduced stress levels, and enhanced mood.
